canonical_definition: GovReport is consistently characterized across the provided literature as a dataset for evaluating language models on long documents, composed of U.S. government and congressional reports. The prevailing use of this benchmark is to measure `Text summarization` capabilities, with papers describing it as a "generic summarization dataset" and a tool to test "long text summarization capacity." Some sources specify its use for abstractive summarization in long-context scenarios. A smaller set of studies uses GovReport to evaluate `Language modeling` performance, particularly for "long sequence language modeling." One paper also positions it as a benchmark for assessing `Length generalization` or "long-context extrapolation." The dataset's documents are noted for their length, with an average of 7,866 tokens, and one source identifies GovReport as part of the SCROLL benchmark.
